original_question:
For numbers from 1 to 1992, calculate the sum of the digits of each number, then calculate the sum of the digits of the resulting 1992 numbers, and so on, until 1992 single-digit numbers are obtained. How many 1s, 9s, and 0s are there among these numbers?

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}
info_removed_type: "the upper limit of the number range"
